creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
title: (No AI) How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
content: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.
contentSnippet: We spoke with several cybersecurity researchers, who look for unknown vulnerabilities and develop tools to exploit them, about how OpenAI’s and Anthropic’s guardrails affect their work.

creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system

contentSnippet: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

title: (No AI) Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Alexa Plus on an Echo Show 15](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/07/8A0A3921.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With [the update](https://developer.amazon.com/alexaplus/blogs/2026/07/alexa-plus-new-ways-to-build-experiences), which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others, while automatically routing requests to the correct device.

In an example shared by Amazon, a person with a supported washing machine can say, "Alexa, my kid's soccer jersey could use a deep clean, but the tag says cold wash only." From there, Alexa Plus would navigate the washer's cycle options and choose the correct setting. The new integrations are powered …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/970399/amazon-alexa-plus-ai-update-smart-home-devices)

With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others, while automatically routing requests to the correct device.
In an example shared by Amazon, a person with a supported washing machine can say, "Alexa, my kid's soccer jersey could use a deep clean, but the tag says cold wash only." From there, Alexa Plus would navigate the washer's cycle options and choose the correct setting. The new integrations are powered  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Claude voice mode running on a phone.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Claude-Voice-2-3-up.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude [Haiku](https://www.theverge.com/news/800007/anthropic-small-model-haiku-4-5), Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its [Opus](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/913184/anthropic-claude-opus-4-7-cybersecurity) and [Sonnet](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/959686/anthropics-claude-sonnet-5-is-here) models available in [voice mode](https://www.theverge.com/news/649175/anthropic-claude-ai-voice-mode-report), and extending its reach into apps like Gmail, Slack, and Canva.

When Anthropic [launched voice mode](https://www.theverge.com/news/675330/anthropics-voice-mode-for-claude-launches-in-beta) last year, it was primarily focused on delivering answers to quick questions with minimal delay. But in a blog post, the company said people immediately started using voice mode for far more than casual queries. They were using it to work "through real business problems," which Haiku was not really designed for. That model "kept conversations quick, but not alwa …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/970065/anthropic-voice-mode-claude-opus-sonnet-haiku-ai)

creator: Boone Ashworth
title: (No AI) Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
dc:creator: Boone Ashworth
content: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.
contentSnippet: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.
